title: Verify Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics Input States
summary: Use the Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen to check which station-related inputs are on and off and review HMI temperature and voltage readings shown on that screen.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when troubleshooting or documenting Hospital HMI station input status from the IO Diagnostics screen, including review of station switch-related input on/off indications and the HMI's displayed temperature and voltage readings.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to assign normal or abnormal meaning to specific temperature values because the source does not provide thresholds.
Do not use this procedure to assign normal or abnormal meaning to specific voltage values because the source does not provide thresholds.
Do not use this procedure as a corrective action procedure because the source only supports observation and documentation of the IO Diagnostics screen.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen, observe station-related input states, review HMI temperature and voltage readings, and record the displayed values for troubleshooting or comparison.

safety_notes: This is a read-only diagnostic observation procedure based on the Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen.
The temperature readings and voltages shown on the IO Diagnostics screen pertain to the HMI itself.
Do not infer acceptable or unacceptable temperature or voltage ranges from this source because no thresholds are provided.

system_preconditions: The Hospital HMI must be available and able to display the IO Diagnostics screen.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI 2.2 IO Diagnostics screen showing station switch input states and HMI temperature and voltage readings.
Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu showing access to 2.2 IO Diagnostics.
Hospital HMI Main Menu for navigation context.

steps.title: Open the Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen
steps.purpose: Access the diagnostic display used to observe station-mounted switch inputs and HMI readings.
steps.instruction: Open the Hospital HMI "IO Diagnostics" screen from the Hospital HMI maintenance area.
steps.expected_result: The Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The IO Diagnostics screen is displayed and its diagnostics area can be viewed.
steps.failure_condition: The IO Diagnostics screen cannot be accessed or is not available on the Hospital HMI.

actions.label: Open IO Diagnostics
actions.exact_action: Open the Hospital HMI "IO Diagnostics" screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Maintenance Menu button set showing access to 2.2 IO Diagnostics.
Hospital HMI screen labeled "IO Diagnostics" with the relevant diagnostics area visible.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required IO Diagnostics screen is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, and 2.4 Default.
The "IO Diagnostics" screen corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.

steps.title: Identify the station switch input indicators
steps.purpose: Locate the input indicators on the IO Diagnostics screen that correspond to the switch mounted on the station.
steps.instruction: Identify the input indicators on the IO Diagnostics screen that correspond to the switch mounted on the station.
steps.expected_result: The relevant station-related input indicators are identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed indicators can be associated with the station-mounted switch context described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed indicators cannot be matched to the station-mounted switch context using the available source information.

actions.label: Locate station input indicators
actions.exact_action: Identify the input indicators that correspond to the switch mounted on the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The area corresponding to the switch mounted on the station and its input indicators.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed input states cannot be matched to the station-mounted switch context using the available source information.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "IO Diagnostics" screen corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.

steps.title: Observe input on and off states
steps.purpose: Determine which station-related inputs are currently shown as active or inactive on the IO Diagnostics screen.
steps.instruction: Observe which identified inputs are shown as on and which are shown as off.
steps.expected_result: The current on/off state of the station-related inputs is observed.
steps.healthy_condition: Input indicators are visible and their on/off state can be read from the screen.
steps.failure_condition: Input indicators are not visible, not readable, or their state cannot be determined from the screen.

actions.label: Read input states
actions.exact_action: Observe which inputs are shown as on and which are shown as off.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The station switch-related input indicators showing which inputs are on and which are off.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required indicators are not available on the IO Diagnostics screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "IO Diagnostics" screen corresponds to the switch mounted on the station and convey which inputs are on and off.

steps.title: Review HMI temperature readings
steps.purpose: Capture the temperature readings displayed on the IO Diagnostics screen and correctly interpret them as HMI readings.
steps.instruction: Review the temperature readings shown on the screen and note that these readings pertain to the HMI itself.
steps.expected_result: The displayed temperature readings are observed and understood to be HMI-related values.
steps.healthy_condition: Temperature readings are visible on the screen and are recognized as pertaining to the HMI itself.
steps.failure_condition: Temperature readings are not visible or cannot be reviewed on the IO Diagnostics screen.

actions.label: Review HMI temperatures
actions.exact_action: Review the temperature readings shown on the screen and note that these readings pertain to the HMI itself.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The temperature readings shown on the IO Diagnostics screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the temperature readings are not available on the IO Diagnostics screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The temperature readings and voltages pertain to the HMI itself.

steps.title: Review HMI voltage readings
steps.purpose: Capture the voltage readings displayed on the IO Diagnostics screen and correctly interpret them as HMI readings.
steps.instruction: Review the voltage readings shown on the screen and note that these readings pertain to the HMI itself.
steps.expected_result: The displayed voltage readings are observed and understood to be HMI-related values.
steps.healthy_condition: Voltage readings are visible on the screen and are recognized as pertaining to the HMI itself.
steps.failure_condition: Voltage readings are not visible or cannot be reviewed on the IO Diagnostics screen.

actions.label: Review HMI voltages
actions.exact_action: Review the voltage readings shown on the screen and note that these readings pertain to the HMI itself.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The voltage readings shown on the IO Diagnostics screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the voltage readings are not available on the IO Diagnostics screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The temperature readings and voltages pertain to the HMI itself.

steps.title: Record observed input, temperature, and voltage values
steps.purpose: Create a documented snapshot of the displayed IO Diagnostics information for troubleshooting or later comparison.
steps.instruction: Record the observed input on/off states and the displayed HMI temperature and voltage values for comparison or troubleshooting.
steps.expected_result: A documented snapshot of the station-related input states and displayed HMI temperature and voltage values is created.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed input states and displayed HMI readings are captured clearly enough for later comparison or troubleshooting.
steps.failure_condition: The observed values cannot be documented because the screen data is unavailable, unclear, or incomplete.

actions.label: Document displayed values
actions.exact_action: Record the observed input on/off states and the displayed HMI temperature and voltage values for comparison or troubleshooting.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Station switch input states together with the HMI temperature and voltage readings to be documented.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required IO Diagnostics indicators or readings are not available for documentation.

The temperature readings and voltages pertain to the HMI itself.

success_criteria: The Hospital HMI IO Diagnostics screen was accessed successfully.
The station-related input indicators were identified and their on/off states were observed.
The displayed HMI temperature readings were reviewed.
The displayed HMI voltage readings were reviewed.
The observed input states and displayed HMI readings were documented for troubleshooting or comparison.
healthy_conditions: The IO Diagnostics screen is available on the Hospital HMI.
The screen shows which station-related inputs are on and off.
Temperature and voltage readings are visible and recognized as pertaining to the HMI itself.
failure_conditions: The required IO Diagnostics screen or indicators are not available.
The observed input states cannot be matched to the station-mounted switch context using the available source information.
Temperature or voltage readings cannot be reviewed on the screen.
Specific temperature or voltage values cannot be judged as normal or abnormal because the source provides no thresholds.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required IO Diagnostics screen or indicators are not available.
Escalate if the observed input states cannot be matched to the station-mounted switch context using the available source information.
Escalate for interpretation of temperature or voltage values if threshold-based judgment is required, because the source does not provide thresholds.

missing_details: The source does not provide explicit navigation steps from the main menu to the IO Diagnostics screen beyond showing that 2.2 IO Diagnostics exists on the Maintenance Menu.
The source does not provide thresholds or acceptable ranges for the displayed temperature readings.
The source does not provide thresholds or acceptable ranges for the displayed voltage readings.
The source does not provide corrective actions based on observed input, temperature, or voltage values.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this diagnostic check.
